title: calceolaria atahualpae
intro: Calceolaria atahualpae, also known as the Atahualpa pocket flower, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountains in Peru. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds. The health benefits of Calceolaria atahualpae include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Calceolaria atahualpae can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for medicinal use, with its leaves and flowers being the primary sources of its bioactive compounds.
